rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical regio- and stereoselective 1,4-carboalkoxylation of 1,3-dienes with Meldrum's acid-type 1,3-dioxan-4,6-diones and alcohols to give (E)-polyfunctionalized allyl ethers
electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis in undivided cell: cathodic reduction of R5OH generates alkoxide (with H2 release); anodic oxidation of deprotonated Meldrum's acid enolate (Eox = 0.44 V vs SCE in DMF) generates an electron-poor C-radical that adds to the diene, followed by a second anodic oxidation of the resulting allyl radical to an allyl cation

key_intermediates.name: Electron-poor C-centered radical from Meldrum's acid (11)
Alkoxide (R5O-) from cathodic reduction of alcohol

electron_flow: Cathode reduces the alcohol to alkoxide + H2; the alkoxide deprotonates Meldrum's acid to give enolate 10, which is anodically oxidized to electron-poor radical 11. Radical 11 adds to the less hindered terminus of the 1,3-diene to give allyl radical 12, which is further oxidized at the anode to allyl cation 13; the alkoxide traps 13 at the 1,4-position to deliver the (E)-allyl ether product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited, consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; BHT-Meldrum's adduct 9 detected by HRMS only when current was applied (with or without diene), supporting anodic generation of radical 11

probe_reagents.name: On/off current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without power supply, even in the presence of MeONa; current is required

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(enolate-MA) = 0.44 V vs SCE; Eox(diene 2a) = 1.81 V and Eox(diene 2d) = 1.16 V vs SCE in DMF — enolate is far easier to oxidize than the dienes, supporting initial anodic oxidation of the enolate rather than the diene

notes: 1,4-regioselectivity (vs the more common 1,2-mode in radical carbooxygenations) is rationalized by an interplay between the EWG character and steric hindrance of the 1,3-dioxan-4,6-dione moiety disfavoring addition at C2, plus a proposed H-bonding directing event from the boat conformation of Meldrum's acid steering alcohol delivery to C4 of cation 11/13. Authors explicitly state further mechanistic studies are underway.
